title: Exterior Modifications Additional Information
description: Please provide detailed information about your exterior modification project to ensure Markland POA compliance and community standards

primary.summary: Comprehensive architectural requirements covering exterior materials, colors, design elements, and approval processes. All exterior modifications must maintain architectural character relevant to climate, history and culture of the area.
keyRequirements: All exterior finishes and color alterations must be reviewed and approved by ARB prior to installation
Colors must be relevant to style and region - vibrant, highly contrasting colors shall be avoided
Minimum two-color scheme required for body and trim
All horizontal bands and accents must wrap around corners
Design consistency required for all exterior elevations to avoid 'stage set' architecture
primary.quote: All exterior finishes and all exterior color alterations must be reviewed and approved by ARB prior to installation... The palette selected must blend and harmonize with surrounding structures.

additionalReferences.summary: Required documentation and approval process for all exterior modifications, with refundable performance deposit requirements
keyProvisions: Completed Design Review Application Form required
Refundable Performance Deposit required (payable to Markland POA)
Architectural plans must depict all exterior elevations with materials and treatments
Colors and materials samples required with digital samples and color names

additionalReferences.summary: Specific requirements for exterior wall finishes, color schemes, and material consistency around the home
keyProvisions: Acceptable materials: stucco, stone, tabby natural shell, brick, wood siding, or manufactured siding (Hardi-Plank type)
Aluminum or vinyl siding prohibited (except fascias and soffits)
Colors in range of pink, purple and black will not be approved
Dominant front elevation materials must wrap around all four sides

additionalReferences.summary: Detailed requirements for doors, windows, shutters, roofing, garage doors, gutters, and other exterior elements
keyProvisions: Entry doors must be stained/painted wood, insulated steel, or fiberglass with glass fenestration
All front-facing windows must incorporate mullion patterns consistent with architectural style
Shutters must be appropriately sized and include proper hardware
All roof penetrations must be painted to match roof or adjacent wall color

fields.label: Primary Type of Modification
fields.description: Select the primary type of exterior modification - ALL require ARB approval

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.4 Exterior Architectural Design Elements & Section 6.4.8 Exterior Materials and Finishes
relevantBylaws.requirement: All exterior modifications require ARB review and approval prior to installation
keyRestrictions: Paint colors must blend and harmonize with surrounding structures
Materials must be consistent with architectural style
Pink, purple, and black colors will not be approved

fields.label: Additional Modifications (if any)
fields.description: Check any additional work - each may require separate ARB approval

fields.label: Paint Colors and Brand
fields.placeholder:
Main Color: [Brand] [Color Name] ([Color Code])
Trim Color: [Brand] [Color Name] ([Color Code])
Accent Color (if applicable): [Brand] [Color Name] ([Color Code])
fields.description: List specific paint colors with manufacturer names and color codes - digital samples required for ARB review

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.4.8D & 2.5C - Color requirements and submission process
requirements: Colors must blend and harmonize with surrounding structures
Pink, purple, and black will not be approved
Minimum two-color scheme required (body and trim)
Maximum two colors on main body (excluding trim, shutters, doors)
Digital samples required with color names for ARB review
relevantBylaws.quote: Colors in the range of pink, purple and black will not be approved on the exterior of a residence. At a minimum a two color scheme must be used for the body and trim.

fields.label: Material Specifications
fields.placeholder: Describe all materials with specific details: siding type/brand, roofing material, window specifications, door materials, etc. Include manufacturer names and model numbers.
fields.description: Include brand names, model numbers, colors, and finish specifications - must comply with approved materials list

relevantBylaws.prohibited: Aluminum or vinyl siding prohibited (fascias and soffits may be painted metal or vinyl)

fields.label: Areas Being Modified
fields.description: Select all areas - dominant materials must wrap around all four sides

relevantBylaws.requirement: Dominant front elevation materials on the front of the home shall generally wrap around all four sides of the home consistent with the architectural character
relevantBylaws.note: All horizontal bands and stone or brick accents on front must wrap around corners and extend minimum 2 feet on sides

fields.label: Approximate Square Footage of Work Area
fields.description: Estimate the total exterior surface area being modified

fields.label: Your Home's Architectural Style
fields.description: Select your home's style - modifications must be consistent with this style

relevantBylaws.requirement: Architecture must be relevant to climate, history and culture of area. No mixing of various architectural styles within one home.
relevantBylaws.quote: The Developer envisions a complementary yet diverse architectural palette for Markland. The Seller's preferred architectural styles are Greek Revival, Coastal Cottage, Spanish Colonial, Mediterranean, Craftsman and Modern Formal Farmhouse.

fields.label: Neighborhood Compatibility Statement
fields.placeholder: Explain how your modifications will complement the existing neighborhood aesthetic and surrounding homes...
fields.description: Critical requirement - ARB evaluates total aesthetic effect on community

relevantBylaws.requirement: ARB evaluates each application for total effect - may deny approval if overall aesthetic impact is unacceptable even if individual criteria are met
relevantBylaws.quote: It is possible therefore, that a proposed improvement might meet the individual criteria delineated in this document and still not receive approval if, in the sole judgment of the Developer or ARB, its overall aesthetic impact is unacceptable.

fields.label: Corner Lot Special Requirements
options: Yes - this is a corner lot
fields.description: Corner lots have additional design requirements for street-facing sides

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.3.5 Houses on Corner Lots & Section 6.4.12A
relevantBylaws.requirement: Side facades facing streets must be designed to adequately address the street with proper proportions and detail. All windows on street-facing sides must incorporate mullion patterns.
relevantBylaws.quote: Side facades, when facing a street, shall be designed to adequately address the street with proper proportions and adequate level of detail.

fields.label: Is your home in a historic district or have historical significance?
fields.description: Historic properties may have additional requirements beyond Markland guidelines

fields.label: Previous Exterior Modifications
fields.placeholder: Describe any previous exterior changes made to the property and their ARB approval status...
fields.description: Include dates, types of modifications, and whether they received proper ARB approval

fields.label: Work Will Be Performed By
fields.description: All work must meet professional standards regardless of who performs it

fields.label: Contractor Company Name
fields.description: Required if using professional contractor

fields.label: Contractor License Number
fields.description: State contractor license number (required if using contractor)

fields.label: Contractor Insurance Information
fields.description: Liability insurance details (required if using contractor)

fields.label: Planned Start Date
fields.description: Work cannot begin until ARB approval is received

relevantBylaws.requirement: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval
relevantBylaws.quote: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval.

fields.label: Expected Completion Date
fields.description: Must complete work within reasonable timeframe to maintain community standards

fields.label: Work Schedule and Hours
fields.placeholder: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM, No Sunday work
fields.description: Must comply with St. Johns County regulations and community standards

relevantBylaws.requirement: Construction hours shall generally comply with St. Johns County regulations and codes. Developer and Association reserve right to limit weekend and holiday hours as residents occupy community.

fields.label: Are building permits required for this work?
fields.description: Check with St. Johns County building department if uncertain

fields.label: Building Permit Number(s)
fields.description: Provide permit numbers if already obtained

fields.label: ARB Application Status
fields.description: ARB approval is MANDATORY before any exterior modification work begins

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 2.1 & Section 6.4.8C - Application and approval requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: All exterior finishes and all exterior color alterations must be reviewed and approved by ARB prior to installation
relevantBylaws.process: Submit completed Design Review Application Form with refundable performance deposit

fields.label: Performance Deposit Status
options: Payment ready to submit with application
fields.description: Refundable performance deposit required with application

relevantBylaws.details: Refundable Performance Deposit payable to Markland POA. Deposit refundable at successful completion and compliance with Design Standards. 90-day correction period for non-compliance.

fields.label: Utility Line Considerations
fields.description: Consider electrical, cable, phone lines attached to exterior

fields.label: Neighbor Notification Status
options: Will inform neighbors before starting work
fields.description: Good neighbor communication recommended for exterior modifications

fields.label: Expected Noise and Disruption Level
options: Moderate - power tools during normal hours
Extensive - structural work with significant noise
fields.description: Must comply with noise regulations and community standards

relevantBylaws.requirement: Owner/Builder will make every effort to keep noise to minimum including compliance with St. Johns County ordinances. Noise and music must be controlled at all times.

fields.label: Property Access Requirements
fields.placeholder: Describe any special access needs, equipment staging areas, etc.
fields.description: All work must remain within property boundaries

relevantBylaws.requirement: All construction activities must be controlled within Owner/Builder's property boundaries. Access on adjacent lots and common areas strictly prohibited.

fields.label: Construction Waste Disposal Plan
fields.placeholder: Describe how construction debris and waste materials will be handled and disposed of...
fields.description: Must maintain clean construction site at all times

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.2.3A & 4.2.3B - Site cleanliness and waste management
requirements: Maintain clean and orderly construction site at all times
Empty refuse bins in timely manner
Clean any debris from neighborhood and community streets
All trash disposed of off-site in compliance with codes

required_documents: Refundable Performance Deposit (amount per application form)
Color samples and material specifications with manufacturer details
Digital samples of exterior colors with color names
Architectural plans showing all exterior elevations with materials
Contractor license and insurance certificates (if using contractor)
Site plan showing modification areas and setbacks
Building permits (if required by St. Johns County)
Product cut sheets or technical specifications

criticalReminders: NO exterior modification work allowed without ARB approval
ALL exterior color changes require ARB review and approval
Performance deposit required with application (refundable upon compliance)
Digital color samples with manufacturer names required for review
Materials must comply with approved list - vinyl/aluminum siding prohibited
Colors in pink, purple, and black range will not be approved
90-day correction period for bringing non-compliant work into conformance
commonViolations: Starting work before receiving ARB approval
Using non-approved colors (pink, purple, black, vibrant colors)
Failing to wrap front facade materials around sides
approvalProcess: Submit completed Design Review Application Form
Pay refundable performance deposit to Markland POA
Provide all required documentation and samples
Wait for ARB review and approval before starting
Complete work according to approved plans
Request final inspection for deposit refund
